justification: All 5+ rules identified with correct invariant classifications. State machine directionality explicitly modeled (BR-2: in_transit->pending forbidden, BR-3: delivered is terminal). 11 acceptance criteria all in Given/When/Then format. Testable assertions are concrete and developer-actionable. Weight bracket correctly marked invariant: false with sound justification. Confidence at 90 is at the upper edge of the 65-90 range but assumptions are clearly stated. BR-5 splits the weight cap as invariant: false (defensible interpretation) while BR-4 covers weight>0 as invariant: true.

observations.text: Identified 6 rules exceeding the minimum 5, with nuanced split of weight validation into positive-check (invariant) and max-cap (policy)
State machine directionality explicitly and correctly modeled with clear irreversibility justifications
All 11 acceptance criteria use strict Given/When/Then format with concrete preconditions
Testable assertions are specific and actionable — e.g., exact boundary values like 9.99 kg, 10 kg, 50 kg
Weight bracket classification correctly marked invariant: false with business reasoning about pricing policy
Edge cases are thorough, including floating-point precision and the Pending-to-Delivered skip scenario
Confidence at 90 is at the ceiling of the acceptable range; the unspecified Pending-to-Delivered skip and mutable-weight questions arguably warrant 80-85
BR-5 marking the 1000 kg max as invariant: false is a debatable call — the rubric expects weight <= 1000 as invariant: true, though the agent's reasoning is coherent
